audit_items.proof_trace: GetBlobFileHandle() returns image->blob->file_info.file, which OpenBlob() legally sets to NULL on success when gzip/bzip2 magic bytes are detected or memory-mapping is used. coders/dot.c:140-142 passes the return value directly to agread() without a NULL check; agread(NULL) causes a NULL pointer dereference. Secondary unchecked callers exist in dps.c:217,230,478,482 and svg.c:3580; info.c:191 is safe because IdentifyImage() internally falls back to stdout on NULL.
audit_items.attack_scenario: Attacker supplies a file whose first three bytes are the gzip magic (0x1F 0x8B 0x08) with a .dot extension; OpenBlob detects the magic, closes file_info.file, sets it to NULL, and returns MagickTrue, after which ReadDOTImage calls agread(NULL), crashing the process.
